architecture_drivers.driver: Support supplier product and service aggregation without making automatic supplier portal sync MVP-critical.
Represent products, services, bundles, recipes, costs, pricing rules, margins, and generated outputs as explicit domains.
Protect supplier credentials, proprietary pricing, customer-specific pricing, and margin data.
Use asynchronous processing for imports, document generation, future sync, and AI-assisted workflows.
Keep AI, customer portals, analytics, and deep integrations outside launch-critical topology.

system_topology.rationale: MVP needs coherent data and workflow velocity more than independently deployable microservices. Explicit module boundaries preserve future extraction paths.

primary_systems.name: Web Application
responsibilities: Pricing, bundle, recipe, and quote workflows
stage_4_handoff_notes: Stage 4 should design task-oriented screens for supplier setup, import review, asset library, pricing, bundle/recipe builder, and quote output.

primary_systems.name: Integration Adapter Layer
responsibilities: Future ecommerce, CRM, accounting, storage, and AI adapters

primary_systems.name: Operational Console
responsibilities: Support visibility without unrestricted secret access
stage_4_handoff_notes: Operational console UX can be lower polish than customer-facing workflows but must support support/debug visibility.

external_systems: Future ecommerce, CRM, accounting, and storage systems

system_boundaries.name: Workspace Trust Boundary
system_boundaries.description: All user-facing requests are scoped to an authenticated workspace and role.

system_boundaries.name: Supplier Integration Boundary
system_boundaries.description: Supplier-specific import, sync, credential, and rate-limit behavior must remain behind adapter interfaces.

system_boundaries.name: Generated Output Boundary
system_boundaries.description: Quotes, catalogs, price books, and proposals are generated from approved source-of-truth data and stored as output metadata plus files.

system_boundaries.name: AI Assistive Boundary
system_boundaries.description: AI suggestions must not directly mutate approved records without human review.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use a modular monolith for MVP with explicit domain modules.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Preserves implementation speed while giving Stage 5 clear ownership boundaries.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use asynchronous jobs for imports and generated outputs.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Supplier files, documents, and future sync/AI work are variable-duration tasks.

risks_and_constraints.risk: A modular monolith can decay into unclear boundaries.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Stage 5 must assign tickets by domain module and enforce domain-owned data access.

risks_and_constraints.risk: Supplier integration scope can expand beyond MVP.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Implement file/manual import first and keep supplier connectors behind adapter contracts.

stage_4_handoff_notes: Design UX around import review and correction, not invisible automation.
Expose quote output as the first generated-output workflow.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Build domain modules before external connector automation.
Use workers for import and document generation from the first implementation slice.
